HOST V1 - Hotel Operations Security Tool

Comprehensive Security Assessment Tool for Hotel Facilities

SECURITY ASSESSMENT PROCESS OVERVIEW

1. Facility Information

Basic facility details, assessor information, and hotel contacts

2. Infrastructure & Dependencies

Utility systems, backup systems, and significant infrastructure

3. Physical Security

Standoff measures, security force, and physical barriers

4. Security Systems

Video surveillance, access control, and monitoring systems

5. VIP Security Planning

VIP access controls and restricted area management

6. Emergency Planning

Emergency procedures, communication systems, and response plans

7. Final Assessment Report

Comprehensive report with findings and recommendations

ASSESSMENT INSTRUCTIONS & GUIDELINES

◊
Assessment Overview

HOST V1 (Hotel Operations Security Tool) is a comprehensive security assessment platform designed specifically for hotel facilities. The tool evaluates physical security, electronic systems, emergency preparedness, and operational security measures to identify vulnerabilities and provide actionable security consideration options.

▤
Assessment Process

Complete each section systematically: Facility Information, Infrastructure & Dependencies, Physical Security, Security Systems, VIP Planning, and Emergency Planning. Each section builds upon the previous one to consider a comprehensive security profile.

◐
Time Requirements

Allow 1-2 hours for a complete assessment depending on facility size and complexity. The tool auto-saves your progress, allowing you to complete the assessment over multiple sessions.

📄
Required Documentation

Gather facility blueprints, security system specifications, emergency procedures, staff training records, vendor contracts, and contact information for key personnel before beginning the assessment.

👥
Assessment Team

Include security personnel, facilities management, emergency response coordinators, and hotel management in the assessment process to ensure comprehensive coverage of all security aspects.

💾
Data Management

Use the data management tools at the bottom of each page to save, load, validate, and export your assessment data. Regular saving ensures no data loss during the assessment process.

Electronic Security Systems

Video surveillance, access control, and electronic security technology

Assessment Approach: This section evaluates electronic security systems including video surveillance, access control, monitoring capabilities, and system integration.

VIDEO SURVEILLANCE SYSTEMS (VSS) VSS = Video Surveillance System. Camera systems used for security monitoring and incident documentation.

Select all that apply

Electronic Locking Systems (ELS) ELS = Electronic Locking System. Digital access control systems that replace traditional keys with cards, fobs, or mobile apps.

Select all that apply

Security Monitoring & Integration

VIP Operations

VIP transportation, logistics, communication, staff, and emergency procedures

Assessment Approach: This section evaluates the facility's capabilities for VIP security services and restricted access areas. The assessment will identify current planning and consideration opportunities.

VIP ACCESS CONTROL & EXTERNAL ENTRANCES

Track VIP entrance types, vehicle access capabilities, and related security measures for each VIP access point. Focus on external entrances where VIPs can arrive by vehicle or on foot, including porte cocheres, valet areas, and dedicated VIP entrances.

Entrance Type Access Type Access Control Method Vehicle Access VSS Coverage Actions

RESTRICTED ACCESS & VIP AREAS

Specify restricted areas (Floor/Area), access control, egress routes, and VSS coverage. These areas are secured using electronic access control devices—such as card readers, keypads or biometrics—integrated with the property management system.

Area Type Location/Floor Rooms Occupancy Access Control Elevator Access Stairwell Exclusion Method VSS Coverage Monitoring Type Actions

RETAIL & COMMERCIAL SPACES SECURITY ASSESSMENT

Assess security measures for all retail and commercial spaces within the hotel property, focusing on their ability to segregate from general public access during elevated risk periods. This includes restaurants, spas, gyms, boutiques, and other commercial considerations.

Space Name Location/Floor Entrance Type Door Lock Technology Open to Public Management Restrictable Access VSS Coverage Actions

VIP TRANSPORTATION & LOGISTICS

Document VIP transportation capabilities, vehicle screening procedures, and logistics support for VIP arrivals and departures.

VIP COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS

Assess VIP-specific communication capabilities and emergency contact procedures.

VIP STAFF & PERSONNEL

Document VIP service staff training, background checks, and specialized personnel assignments.

VIP EMERGENCY PROCEDURES

Document VIP-specific emergency procedures, evacuation plans, and medical emergency protocols.

VIP TECHNOLOGY & SYSTEMS

Assess VIP-specific technology systems, access control, and monitoring capabilities.
